title: Adenosma bilabiata
taxonomy: Adenosma bilabiata is classified within the plant family Plantaginaceae. This family is a part of the larger order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further grouped into the clade Asterids, part of the larger clade Angiosperms. The class of flowering plants is Magnoliopsida, which is a group of angiosperms. Adenosma bilabiata is also classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons in its seeds.
uses:

It has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory ailments.

intro: Adenosma bilabiata, also known as 'Kokilaksha' or 'Heart-leafed Adenosma', is a plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and fragrant, white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Adenosma bilabiata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
